name: Platform Health Check
description: T0 smoke test — confirm sandbox, search, and file tools work before running any benchmarks
prompt:
Complete these 3 quick health checks and save results to health-check.json:
1. Execute Python code: print(2 + 2) — save stdout
2. Search the web for 'today date' — save the first result snippet
3. Write a file hello.txt containing 'AI Partner is healthy'
Save all 3 results to health-check.json with keys: python_ok (bool), search_ok (bool), file_ok (bool), timestamp (ISO string).

name: Sandboxed Python Execution
description: Generates 50 Fibonacci numbers in Docker, saves JSON + stdout
prompt:
Write a Python script that:
1. Generates the first 50 Fibonacci numbers
2. Calculates their sum and average
3. Saves results to fibonacci-results.json with keys: numbers (array), sum (number), average (number)
4. Prints exactly "DONE" to stdout

Execute the script. Save the stdout output to fibonacci-stdout.txt.

config.criteria: The file contains exactly 50 Fibonacci numbers in the 'numbers' array and the 'sum' value equals their correct sum (12586269025)

name: TypeScript Compile Validation
description: Write a TS file with correct types, compile with tsc, validate output JS exists
prompt:
Create a TypeScript file at greeting.ts that:
- Exports a function greet(name: string): string
- Returns the string: Hello, {name}! Welcome to AI Partner.
- Has proper TypeScript type annotations

Compile it with: npx tsc greeting.ts --target ES2020 --module commonjs
Save any compilation output (stdout+stderr) to compile-log.txt.

config.criteria: The file exports a function named 'greet' that accepts a 'name' parameter of type string and returns a string type. The function returns a greeting string containing the name.

name: Wikipedia Extract + Structured Report
description: Navigate Wikipedia AI article, extract content, screenshot, create structured report
prompt:
Go to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence

Extract:
- The full introduction section text
- The list of AI subfields from the table of contents
- The first paragraph of the History section

Save raw extraction to wiki-ai-raw.txt.

Create report.md with:
- 5 key facts about AI (numbered list)
- 3 major subfields with one-line description each
- A 100-word summary of AI history

Take a full-page screenshot and save as wiki-ai.png.

config.criteria: The file has at least 5 numbered facts about AI, mentions at least 3 subfields with descriptions, and contains a history summary paragraph of approximately 100 words. Content is factually consistent with Wikipedia.

name: GitHub Trending Scrape
description: Navigate GitHub trending, extract top 10 repos, screenshot, create summary
prompt:
Go to https://github.com/trending?since=daily&spoken_language_code=en

Extract the top 10 trending repositories. For each repo get:
- Repository name in owner/name format
- Stars gained today (number only)
- Total stars count
- Primary programming language
- Description
- Full URL

Save all data to trending.json as an array of objects with keys: name, stars_today, total_stars, language, description, url.

Take a screenshot and save as github-trending.png.

Create trending-summary.md with a one-line highlight per repository.

config.criteria: The file contains at least 8 repository entries (ideally 10). Each entry has a non-empty name in owner/name format (contains a slash), and stars_today is a non-negative number.

name: HN Front Page Newsletter
description: Scrape HN, extract 15 stories, create a formatted newsletter with editor's pick
prompt:
Go to https://news.ycombinator.com

Extract the top 15 stories: rank, title, URL, points, comment count.
Save to hn-data.json as an array with keys: rank, title, url, points, comments.

Create daily-brief.md formatted as a newsletter:
- Header with today's date (2026-03-06)
- Each story as a numbered item with title, points, and one sentence about why it matters
- A final "Editor's Pick" section with your top story and a 3-sentence justification

Take a screenshot and save as hn-front.png.

config.criteria: The file reads like a newsletter: has a date header, at least 10 numbered stories each with a commentary sentence, and a final Editor's Pick section with a named story and multi-sentence justification.

name: Dynamic Tool Creation + Use
description: Create a fetch_weather tool, register it, use it for 3 cities
prompt:
Create a reusable Python script tool called fetch_weather.py that:
- Accepts a city name as a command-line argument
- Fetches weather from https://wttr.in/{city}?format=j1 (free JSON API, no auth needed)
- Prints a JSON object with: city, temperature_C, condition, humidity
- Handles errors gracefully

Save the script to fetch_weather.py.

Then run it for 3 cities using run_command:
1. python3 fetch_weather.py Delhi
2. python3 fetch_weather.py London
3. python3 fetch_weather.py "New York"

Save all 3 outputs combined into weather-report.json as an array.
Create weather-summary.md comparing the 3 cities with a simple table.

config.criteria: The file contains weather data for at least 2 of the 3 cities (Delhi, London, New York). Each entry has a temperature value and a condition/description field.

name: Multi-Agent Delegation
description: Orchestrator delegates Express API to coder agent, writes docs in parallel
prompt:
Complete this task using parallel agent delegation:

Delegate to a coder agent:
"Create a REST API in Express.js at server.js with these endpoints:
- GET /api/health → returns {status: 'ok', timestamp: Date.now()}
- GET /api/agents → returns [{name: 'ReActAgent'}, {name: 'CoderAgent'}, {name: 'ResearchAgent'}]
- POST /api/task → accepts {task: string}, returns {id: '<uuid>', status: 'queued', task: task}
Install dependencies with npm install express."

While the coder agent works, you create api-docs.md with:
- Title: AI Partner API Documentation
- Section for each endpoint: method, URL, request body (if any), response format, example curl command

After both complete: run node server.js in the background for 5 seconds, then test with:
- curl http://localhost:3000/api/health
Save the curl response to health-response.json.

config.criteria: The documentation covers all 3 endpoints (/api/health, /api/agents, /api/task) with HTTP method, URL, and at least one curl example each.

name: SQLite Schema + Small Model
description: Create SaaS DB schema, execute with sqlite3, verify all 3 tables exist
prompt:
Create a complete SQLite database schema for a SaaS application.

Save the following SQL to schema.sql:
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    role TEXT DEFAULT 'user'
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS subscriptions (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    plan TEXT NOT NULL,
    status TEXT DEFAULT 'active',
    expires_at DATETIME
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    user_id TEXT,
    action TEXT NOT NULL,
    metadata TEXT,
    timestamp DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
```

Execute: sqlite3 saas.db < schema.sql
Verify tables: sqlite3 saas.db ".tables" and save output to tables-verification.txt.

name: Telegram Notification Pipeline
description: 3 sequential tasks with Telegram notification after each
prompt:
Complete 3 tasks in sequence and send a Telegram notification after each:

Task 1: Fetch https://news.ycombinator.com, extract the top story title and URL. Send Telegram message: "✅ Task 1 done — Top HN story: {title} — {url}"

Task 2: Write a Python script reverse.py that reverses a string passed as command-line argument. Run it with input 'AgentForge'. Send Telegram message: "✅ Task 2 done — reversed = {output}"

Task 3: Create summary.md that combines both results: the HN story and the reverse output. Send Telegram: "✅ All 3 tasks complete — summary.md is ready."

Save all results to pipeline-results.json with keys: task1 (title+url), task2 (reversed_string), task3 (status).

config.criteria: The file has a task1 entry with a non-empty title and url, a task2 entry with a reversed string, and a task3 entry indicating completion.

name: Document Generation Suite
description: Generate PDF, XLSX, and PPTX from GitHub trending data in one goal
prompt:
Fetch fresh data from https://github.com/trending (navigate with browser).

Extract top 10 repos: rank, name (owner/name), language, stars today, total stars, description.

Generate 3 documents and save them to a trending-docs/ folder:

1. trending-report.html — A professional HTML report with:
   - Title: "GitHub Trending Report — 2026-03-06"
   - Summary paragraph about trending languages
   - Formatted table of top 10 repos

2. trending-data.xlsx — Excel file with columns: Rank, Repo Name, Language, Stars Today, Total Stars, Description

3. trending-slides.pptx — 5-slide PowerPoint:
   - Slide 1: Title 'GitHub Trending Report 2026-03-06'
   - Slides 2-4: Top 3 repos with name, description, star count
   - Slide 5: 'Key Insights' with 3 bullet points about trending tech

config.criteria: The file is valid HTML containing a table of GitHub repos with at least 5 rows, each with a repo name, language, and star count.

name: Stuck Loop Recovery
description: Agent hits fake API, auto-recovers by finding real alternative
prompt:
Attempt to fetch stock price data:

1. Try fetching: https://api.fakebroker-xyz-notreal.com/v1/stocks/AAPL
2. If that fails (it will), try up to 2 more times with 2-second delays
3. After all retries fail, search the web for a free stock price API that requires no authentication
4. Use that alternative API to get the current AAPL stock price
5. Save to stock-price.json with keys: symbol ('AAPL'), price (number), source_api (URL), timestamp (ISO)
6. Screenshot any financial page you visit as stock-page.png

Note: Do NOT exceed 3 retry attempts on the fake URL. Move on quickly.

config.criteria: The file has an AAPL price that is a reasonable stock price number (between 50 and 1000), a source_api field with a real URL, and a timestamp.

name: Full Autonomous Daily Brief
description: Hero demo: 3 sites, 5 files, full browser + synthesis pipeline
prompt:
You are my autonomous research assistant. Complete ALL steps without stopping:

STEP 1 — GitHub (https://github.com/trending?since=daily)
Extract top 5 AI/ML repos: name, stars today, description, URL.

STEP 2 — Hacker News (https://news.ycombinator.com)
Extract top 5 stories: title, points, comment count, URL.

STEP 3 — arXiv (https://arxiv.org/list/cs.AI/recent)
Extract latest 3 papers: title, authors list, abstract page URL.

STEP 4 — Create:
- daily-brief.json with structure: { repos: [...], news: [...], papers: [...], generated_at: '<ISO timestamp 2026-03-06T...' }
- daily-brief.md formatted newsletter:
  Header: "🤖 AI Partner Daily Brief — March 6, 2026"
  📦 GitHub Trending section (5 repos)
  📰 Hacker News section (5 stories)
  📄 arXiv section (3 papers)
  ⭐ Editor's Pick section with 1 story from any source and 3-sentence justification

STEP 5 — Screenshot each site:
- github.png (GitHub trending page)
- hn.png (HN front page)
- arxiv.png (arXiv cs.AI listing)

config.criteria: The file has a 'repos' array with at least 3 entries, a 'news' array with at least 3 entries, and a 'papers' array with at least 2 entries. All entries have non-empty titles/names.

name: Full Stack App Generation
description: Build complete Node.js REST API with tests that actually pass
prompt:
Build a complete working Node.js REST API:

1. Create package.json with:
   - name: ai-partner-api
   - dependencies: express, uuid
   - devDependencies: jest, supertest
   - scripts: test: jest --testTimeout=10000

2. Create server.js with Express:
   - GET /api/health → { status: 'ok', timestamp: Date.now() }
   - GET /api/agents → [{ name: 'ReActAgent', role: 'reasoner' }, { name: 'CoderAgent', role: 'executor' }, { name: 'ResearchAgent', role: 'researcher' }]
   - POST /api/task → accepts { task: string }, returns { id: '<uuid v4>', status: 'queued', task: <echoed back> }
   - Export the app (module.exports = app) for testing

3. Create tests/api.test.js with jest + supertest tests for all 3 endpoints

4. Create README.md with curl examples for all 3 endpoints

Run: npm install && npm test
Save test output to test-output.txt.

config.criteria: The README has curl command examples for at least 2 of the 3 endpoints (/api/health, /api/agents, /api/task).
